ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FIRST ACCOUNTING OF TRUSTEE
Off Calendar Mar-17-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Mraule for (trustee) Michael McLaughlin.

Mr. Mraule reported that he has been corresponding with the examiner, that the only thing outstanding is the fee paid to the 3rd party, that he does not believe a court order is needed for that as it is not being paid to the attorney or paralegal and asked for clarification as to why the court order was necessary. The court explained the need for prior court authorization when a trustee who is not requesting fees uses trust funds for payment, that the accounting is the duty of the trustee and that the court would allow this accounting but not approve it. The Court granted the petition for accounting (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Mraule for and with Michael McLaughlin (Trustee).

The Court informed Counsel that written response to the Probate Examiner's notes is needed and raised the issue of payment to the accountant without Court approval. Mr. Mraule stated he believes they have responded to the prior notes, that the current notes only had one issue, that he was not aware of the payments to the accountant, that he will look into the issues and that his Paralegal has sent an e-mail to the Probate Examiner requesting copies of all notes. The order to appear is vacated. The accounting petition was continued to February 3, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for written responses to the notes and the issues raised.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Park. (Not Reported)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-13-2015 Vacated

Off Calendar Nov-25-2014 Continued To Jan-13-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court noted that the Probate Examiner has indicated that there are some issues with the accounting and that a prior order to appear was issued which included an incorrect hearing date which is not enforceable. An new Order to Appear will be issued to the Trustee and the Attorney to appear on January 13, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they have not responded to the Probate Examiner's notes. The accounting petition is also continued to that date. The prior order to appear is off calendar.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-14-2011 Continued To Apr-04-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kevin Urbatsch for Lawrence Weiss.

Mr. Urbatsch requested a continuance. The Court stated that it needed the original statements, form CG-400; the mandatory form regarding the summary of account, and that it needed information regarding the disbursements (videos games, etc.). Mr Urbatsch stated that he contacted Mr. Weiss about the disbursements, that Mr. Weiss was looking into them, and that Ms. Durston had been out ill so he did not have a response as to the attorney's fees. The Court requested Counsel file a supplemental declaration with the required attachments which should include information regarding the disbursements, the GC-400 form, and the original statements. The Court continued the matter to April 4, 2011 at at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel is to have the supplemental declaration on file by March 30, 2011. The Court directed Mr. Urbatsch to respond to the Examiner's notes in the future.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Order To Establish & Fund Special Needs Trust; Invest Trust Assets In Mutual Funds & Government Bonds Maturing After Five Years
Off Calendar Jul-14-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Linda Durston for and with Valerie Thames. Aisha Roach, (Daughter) was also present.

Ms. Durston submitted a copy of the proof of disability and written consent of the Trust Protector for the Court to review. The Court comfirmed that Ms. Roach consented to serve and stated that the bond amount was set at $196,000.00. Ms. Thames confirmed that she consented to payment of fees in the amount of $6,259.00 to Ms. Durston, to the appointment of Mr. Weiss as Trustee, to the appointment of Mr. McLaughlin as Successor Trustee and naming Ms. Johnson as the Successor Trust Protector. The Court granted the petition, (subject to review of the language in the special needs trust/proposed order).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR#7489. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
